mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and declining consumer spending power, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a walk-forward t-test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and capital formation, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when tested out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which boosts short-term equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following industrial production acceleration, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in a 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the inflation threshold shows no predictive edge (Rank IC < 0.02) across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings growth, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following GDP growth, or the series shows zero predictive correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) in a walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising consumer prices indicates stagflationary supply constraints that erode corporate margins and trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to generate a statistically significant negative edge (p > 0.10) in rolling 3-year tests.
